pr.title: Rest of ftSk_SpecialHi functions almost completely matching
pr.author: Gelatart

summary: Implemented several previously stubbed Sheik SpecialHi, Sheik SpecialLw, and Yoshi SpecialN functions, with the PR author noting only slight remaining instruction differences for some ftSk_SpecialHi functions. The main work was matching Sheik Up-B travel/fall transition logic and related velocity preservation/restoration, while also refining ftSeakAttributes layout so later fields such as x54, x60, x64, x68, and x6C are typed as f32 and usable by matched physics code.
what_changed: Replaced the nonmatching marker for ftSk_SpecialHi_80113A30 with a C implementation for AS_SheikUpBTravelAir that reads the left stick, computes stick magnitude with sqrtf, clamps the value, conditionally flips/normalizes facing with ftCommon_8007D9FC or ftCommon_8007DA24, computes launch angle with atan2f, sets specialhi velocity fields, assigns self_vel using cosf/sinf and ftSeakAttributes x44/x48, changes motion state to 0x167, updates animation, sets anim rate to 0, and calls inlineA0.

Added ftSk_SpecialHi_80113EAC and ftSk_SpecialHi_80113F68 plus local inline helpers that preserve current self/gr velocities into mv.co fields, zero gr_vel/self_vel, clear x221E_b0, set accessory4_cb to fn_80113038, change to motion states 0x165 or 0x168, and restore scaled ground or air velocity using ftSeakAttributes x54.
Implemented ftSk_SpecialLw_Phys as a direct call to ft_80084F3C and ftSk_SpecialAirLw_Phys using ftCommon_8007D494 with attributes x68/x6C followed by ftCommon_8007CEF4.

Expanded and corrected ftSeakAttributes: x54 changed from s32 to f32, x60/x64/x68/x6C added as f32 fields, and data_filler_1 shrunk to 0x04. Obsolete comments about fields matching only when typed f32 were removed after the struct was made concrete.

Added Yoshi SpecialN matches: fn_8012D360 and fn_8012D4F0 transition through ftCommon_8007D7FC and Fighter_ChangeMotionState to states 0x15B and 0x15D; ftYs_SpecialAirN1_0_Anim exits via ftCo_800CC730 when no frames remain; ftYs_SpecialAirN1_0_Coll and ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Coll call ft_80082C74/fn_8012D1AC and ft_8008403C/fn_8012D298 respectively.
Updated ftYs_SpecialN.h prototypes for fn_8012D1AC and fn_8012D4F0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed void callbacks with ftYs_GObj* or Fighter_GObj* parameters.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helper functions in ftSk_SpecialHi.c to reproduce repeated velocity-save/zero/accessory-callback setup for two closely related Sheik Up-B transitions while preserving per-state differences after the helper call.
Kept stack-padding dummy arrays such as u8 _[32], u8 _[4], and u8 _[8] where needed, indicating a matching tactic for stack layout and instruction ordering rather than purely semantic cleanup.

Converted ftSeakAttributes filler into explicit f32 fields only where newly matched code needed them, allowing SpecialHi and SpecialLw physics to access typed attributes instead of relying on opaque padding.
Used existing common fighter helpers for collision/physics state transitions rather than duplicating logic: ft_80084F3C, ftCommon_8007D494, ftCommon_8007CEF4, ft_80082C74, and ft_8008403C.
Preserved existing motion constants and flags from the target code path, including Fighter_ChangeMotionState IDs 0x165, 0x167, 0x168, 0x15B, and 0x15D and Yoshi flag 0x0C4C5092U.
decomp_lessons: For fighter attribute structs, matching several related move functions can reveal that apparent filler should become typed fields; here Sheik attributes beyond x54 were resolved as f32 fields used by SpecialLw air physics.

When a transition function must carry velocity through a motion-state change, storing self_vel/gr_vel into mv.co scratch fields before zeroing and then restoring/scaling afterward can be the pattern needed for a match.

Repeated near-identical transition setup can be factored into static helper functions when it matches inlining behavior, but the helper body must preserve exact field writes such as x221E_b0 and accessory4_cb.

Joystick-vector code may need explicit intermediate locals and clamping order to match: square x/y, sqrt magnitude, clamp to 1.0-like constants, compare against an attribute threshold, and only then compute atan2f with facing_dir.

Header prototype cleanup is part of matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete callback signatures lets newly implemented collision and state-change helpers typecheck and document expected gobj types.
naming_conventions: Sheik files use the internal character prefix ftSk even though the path is ftSeak, e.g. ftSk_SpecialHi_80113A30 and ftSk_SpecialLw_Phys.
Action-state comments identify semantic move states, such as AS_SheikUpBTravelAir and AS_SheikUpBFall, while function names remain address-based until better names are known.
Local helper names were formed by appending _inline to the target function name, e.g. ftSk_SpecialHi_80113EAC_inline and ftSk_SpecialHi_80113F68_inline.
Unresolved attribute fields remained offset-style names such as x40, x44, x48, x54, x68, and x6C rather than speculative semantic names.
Yoshi SpecialN callbacks retained address-style names such as fn_8012D360 and fn_8012D4F0, with typed prototypes added only where evidence supported them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted explicit unused stack arrays to influence stack frame shape: u8 _[32] in ftSk_SpecialHi_80113A30, u8 _[4] in ftSk_SpecialAirLw_Phys, and u8 _[8] in ftYs_SpecialAirN1_0_Anim.
Used chained assignment fp->gr_vel = fp->self_vel.x = fp->self_vel.y = ftSk_Init_804D9660 in Sheik Up-B helpers, likely to match store ordering or compact generated code.
Accessed Fighter data both as gobj->user_data and through GET_FIGHTER depending on the surrounding function style and matching needs.

Kept math-library calls sqrtf, atan2f, cosf, and sinf in the Sheik Up-B travel implementation, with separate locals for stick_y_sq, stick_mag, var_f30, and var_f31 to control generated floating-point code.
Used existing float constants such as ftSk_Init_804D9660, ftSk_Init_804D9664, ftSk_Init_804D9688, ftSk_Init_804D968C, and ftYs_Init_804D9A38 rather than literal floating-point values.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says the ftSk_SpecialHi functions were almost completely matching, with only slight instruction differences remaining on some functions.

The diff includes comments crediting Grumpy Zebra's fork of Gelatart's scratch and kipcode66 context from decomp.me for earlier SpecialHi-related matching work; there is no direct reviewer instruction attached to these comments in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftSk_SpecialHi_80113A30 to see whether the slight instruction differences mentioned in PR #1647 were fully resolved.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic documentation of ftSeakAttributes fields x40 through x6C.
Look for PRs that replace ftSk_SpecialHi_80113EAC_inline or ftSk_SpecialHi_80113F68_inline with shared common helpers or renamed transition routines.
Check later ftYs_SpecialN work for typed prototypes of fn_8012D1AC, fn_8012D360, fn_8012D4F0, and related collision callbacks.
